summary: Iter 6 verified: header anchor-nav, scroll-to-section behavior, pillars section, HeroChatDemo (mock-routed chat) and route guards all work. Backend 4/4 PASS. 35/36 frontend checks PASS. ONE CRITICAL BUG: the StrictMode guard claimed in the review request is NOT implemented in HeroChatDemo.tsx — the seeded assistant reply ('You have 4 casual leaves...') is duplicated on mount (assistant message count == 2, expected 1). Clearly visible in mobile screenshot. All other behavior (nav testids, smooth-scroll to #capabilities / #how-it-works / #pillars / #try-it, 4 pillar cards with exact titles, keyword routing wfh→Hybrid Work Policy, payslip→27 Feb 2026, fallback→RAG/n8n, quick chip click adds reply, mobile no-overflow, header-nav display:none on mobile, /app/dashboard→/sign-in redirect, /sign-in renders) is correct.

ui_bugs.issue: Seeded assistant reply about leaves is rendered TWICE on landing page mount. React StrictMode double-invokes the empty-deps useEffect at lines 62-66 of /app/frontend/src/components/common/HeroChatDemo.tsx and there is NO guard ref despite the PRD claim ('Guard ref added to HeroChatDemo so React StrictMode double-effect doesn't duplicate the seeded reply'). Result: two assistant bubbles with '4 casual leaves and 8 earned leaves...' both showing the same citation 'India-Leave-v3.2.pdf · §4.1 · p.12' — visually wrong and confusing on mobile (visible in screenshot). Assistant message count is 2 after settle; expected 1.

critical_code_review_comments: HeroChatDemo.tsx line 62-66: the comment says 'Stream the seeded reply once on mount' but in React 18 StrictMode (CRA dev + many prod builds), the empty-deps effect fires twice, producing two assistant bubbles. The PRD explicitly claims a guard ref was added — it is not in the committed code. This is a P0 polish bug because it is the very first thing a visitor sees in the hero.

HeroChatDemo.tsx line 94-102 send(): no abort/cancellation if user spams quick chips while a previous reply is still streaming. busy gate at line 96 prevents new sends while streaming, which is good — but the seeded mount-time stream also sets busy=true, so the user briefly cannot type for ~2s after page load. Acceptable for demo but worth noting.
HeroChatDemo.tsx renderBold (line 122) does naive ** parsing — fine for canned content, but if any future canned text contains a stray '**' the parser will mismatch. Low priority.
LandingPage.tsx unchanged from iter 5 review otherwise — useLayoutEffect for the 'dark' class is now correctly applied (line 14). Good fix from iter 5 feedback.

success_rate.frontend: 97% (35/36) — only failure is the StrictMode duplicate seeded reply

test_credentials: Not required — only public surface tested.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent adds the guard ref in HeroChatDemo.tsx, re-run the playwright check that counts elements matching [data-testid^="demo-msg-ai-"] right after the seeded stream settles (~3.5s after load). Expected count: 1. All other 35 checks already pass and do not need re-running unless the file changes. Backend regression suite /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is green. Chat backend remains MOCKED (keyword-routed canned responses for leave/wfh/payslip/fallback) — flagged in the review request.
rca of the issue:
Bug: Seeded assistant reply duplicated under the orb on landing page.
Root cause: /app/frontend/src/components/common/HeroChatDemo.tsx lines 62-66 contains an empty-deps useEffect that calls streamAssistant() without any guard. React 18 StrictMode (and even some HMR scenarios in prod-like builds) double-invokes empty-deps mount effects, so streamAssistant runs twice → two assistant bubbles are appended via setMessages. The PRD entry 6 says 'Guard ref added' but no useRef-based guard exists in the committed file.
Reproduction: Load https://zensar-hr-connect.preview.emergentagent.com/?b=v7, wait 3 seconds, count elements [data-testid^="demo-msg-ai-"] — returns 2 (also visually obvious on mobile screenshot).
Mitigation: Add a useRef boolean flag set on first run and early-return on subsequent runs (see action_items).
